name: Lorehold Spirits
tagline: Reveillark + Karmic Guide + Goblin Bombardment loop (table)
idea: The engine is graveyard cycling, and the engine is the token engine.

briefKills.line: Quintorius −4 (ready ~1 turn after landing) + 4+ Spirits (Akroma's Will / anthems pile on) → Spirits gain double strike + vigilance → lethal alpha across the table
Purphoros + Anointed Procession + a recursion turn (3 events) → 2→4 dmg to EACH opp per Spirit ETB → ~12 to each opp passively

openings: Weakest axis — Durability (4/5): the judges' soft spot.
3-card loop is a side line; fast clock = spirits/Quintorius (was FINDING, -4pp front7).
Decap never-closes in 1% of goldfish games — a grind risk.
Tables (all three) never-close 5% of the time.

clock.caption: Decap (one opponent) lands around T8; the full table around T10.

gamePlan: The engine is graveyard cycling, and the engine is the token engine. Cards entering the graveyard via discard, sacrifice, death, or self-mill set up Quintorius's static; cards leaving the graveyard via recursion fire it. Every recursion event is a Spirit token. Anointed Procession doubles each Spirit. Purphoros pings 2 per Spirit ETB. The deck is designed to feed itself.
winLine: Reveillark + Karmic Guide + Goblin Bombardment loop (table)

finishers.name: Quintorius Ultimate + Akroma's Will
finishers.note: Activate Quintorius's −4 with 4+ Spirits in play (most turns of the mid-game).

finishers.name: Purphoros + Procession + Recursion Turn
finishers.note: With Purphoros and Anointed Procession both in play, every Quintorius static trigger creates 2 Spirits = 4 damage to each opponent from Purphoros.

finishers.name: Moonshaker Cavalry Burst
finishers.note: Moonshaker Cavalry ETB gives each creature flying + counter.

finishers.name: Boros Charm 4 Damage Mode
finishers.note: Sometimes the kill is "Boros Charm 4 damage" to finish a low-life opponent at instant speed.

finishers.name: Spirit Swarm + Anthem Grindout
finishers.note: Patchwork Banner, Balefire Liege (+ 3 dmg per RW spell cast), and Tocasia's Welcome (draw) provide a 3–4 turn grindout when Quintorius and Moonshaker are both unavailable.

finishers.name: Reveillark / Karmic Guide / Goblin Bombardment Combo

rulings.name: Quintorius is a PLANESWALKER, not a creature.
rulings.note: He survives creature wipes (Wrath, Tragic Arrogance) but dies to combat damage and direct damage — and cannot be protected by Selfless Spirit / Guardian of Faith.

rulings.name: The static fires once per event
rulings.note: — "one or more cards leave your graveyard" = ONE Spirit, even when two cards leave at once (Reveillark returning two = 1 token, not 2). Many separate events across a turn is how you stack tokens.

rulings.name: Hofri exiles dying nontoken creatures — that exile does NOT trigger Quintorius;
rulings.note: the trigger fires later when the Hofri-token's cards hit the graveyard. Hofri also interferes with the Reveillark loop (Reveillark gets exiled instead of going to the yard) — remove/sac Hofri first, or use Hofri Spirit tokens as the sac fuel.

rulings.name: Table-check ruling
rulings.note: whether tokens dying (briefly hitting the yard, then ceasing to exist) triggers the static. Mainstream answer is yes — meaning dying Hofri Spirits and sacrificed Treasures each fire it. If a judge rules no, several multipliers contract but the engine still runs off real-card recursion.

rulings.name: Goblin Bombardment is an enchantment, not a creature
rulings.note: "Sacrifice a creature: 1 damage to any target," with no sac cost beyond the creature. It's the loop's free sac outlet; protect it like any combo piece (no in-deck recursion for it).

keep.mixed: 3-card loop is a side line; fast clock = spirits/Quintorius (was FINDING, -4pp front7)

mull.strategy: Looking for: ramp + a discard/recursion piece + a route to Quintorius by T4. A lone Reveillark-loop piece (Reveillark / Karmic Guide / Goblin Bombardment) is NOT a keep on its own — the loop is a late side line. The spirits/Quintorius engine shape is the keep.

killTree.title: Lorehold Spirits — Quintorius, History Chaser
killTree.root:
Quintorius (planeswalker) resolved
each time cards LEAVE your graveyard → a 3/2 Spirit (one per event)
killTree.stall:
run Quintorius +1 to fill the yard + dig; deploy recursion + a multiplier —
every recursion event is a Spirit, so build toward the −4 alpha
killTree.caption: cheapest line first · fall to the next

lines.need:
Quintorius −4 (ready ~1 turn after landing) + 4+ Spirits
(Akroma's Will / anthems pile on)
lines.kill: Spirits gain double strike + vigilance → lethal alpha across the table

lines.need:
Purphoros + Anointed Procession
+ a recursion turn (3 events)
lines.kill: 2→4 dmg to EACH opp per Spirit ETB → ~12 to each opp passively

lines.need:
Moonshaker Cavalry ETB
+ a wide Spirit board (Procession / Patchwork)
lines.kill: all creatures gain flying + a counter → one-turn lethal swing on one opp

lines.need:
Reveillark + Karmic Guide + Goblin Bombardment
+ a ≤2-power creature in yard
lines.kill: unbounded sac loop → arbitrary GB damage + arbitrary Spirits

lines.need:
Patchwork Banner + Balefire Liege + Tocasia's Welcome
(Quintorius & Moonshaker down) (fallback)
